title: Roaming Partner Settlement and Inter-Carrier Billing Verification
dataSources: `index=roaming` hosting `sourcetype=tap:record` (TAP3 **RAP** file records after internal parser) and/or `sourcetype=nrtrde:record` for **NRTRDE** near-real-time fraud/steering feeds, cross-compared with `index=cdr` `sourcetype=cdr:roaming` (home PLMN rated roaming events). **Index patterns**: segregate **clearinghouse** files (BICS, Syniverse, Comfone) from **internal GTP/SGSN** exports to control retention and access. **Input mechanisms**: batch **TAP3**/**RAP** files via `monitor://` into a HF with **custom `props.conf`** (fixed-width or BER TLV parsing often needs `Splunk TA` bespoke add-on or `SEDCMD` pre-processing); **NRTRDE** often hourly batches via SFTP; internal rated roaming via **DB Connect** from mediation. **Key fields** for this UC: `roaming_partner`, `imsi`, `visited_network`, `charge_amount`, `record_type` (MOC/MTC/GPRS), `billing_period`, plus file **sequence** / **release** number when available. **Volume**: TAP files are bursty (millions of records per drop per partner); plan **multi-GB per file** peak after roaming season; NRTRDE lower volume but higher velocity for fraud teams. **Parsing notes**: align **MCC/MNC** vs commercial partner codes via lookup; normalise **currency** and **tax** rounding (six decimals in clearinghouse workflows); TAP **duplicate detection** uses release numbers — ingest metadata `tap_file_sequence` as indexed field for Splunk correlation.
app: Deploy **heavy forwarder** with `monitor:///opt/roaming/TAP/incoming/*.rap` (or `.out` per hub convention) and companion `props.conf` transforming binary-friendly intermediate CSV if an external converter is used. Use **Splunk DB Connect** for curated `cdr:roaming` extracts from mediation when TAP is not yet available. For NRTRDE, schedule frequent small-batch polls. **Do not** mix TAP binary directly without a supported parser — use clearinghouse-provided ASCII **CDR layouts** or vendor middleware. Optional: **Kafka** ingestion for real-time TAP streaming feeds from modernization programs.
spl:
index=roaming sourcetype=tap:record
| stats count as tap_events, sum(charge_amount) as tap_charges by roaming_partner, billing_period
| join type=left roaming_partner, billing_period [
    search index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:roaming
    | stats count as cdr_events, sum(rated_amount) as cdr_charges by roaming_partner, billing_period
  ]
| eval record_gap=tap_events-cdr_events, charge_diff=round(tap_charges-cdr_charges, 2)
| where abs(record_gap) > 100 OR abs(charge_diff) > 1000
| sort -abs(charge_diff)
description: Compares transferred-account-procedure style partner charge files against home-network roaming rated events by partner and billing period, surfacing record-count drift and charge mismatches ahead of clearinghouse submission deadlines.
value: Settlement teams avoid surprise debit notes and arbitration by fixing file gaps and rating disagreements while partners still accept revisions; that protects roaming margin and keeps regulatory and inter-operator commitments on schedule.
implementation: Normalize partner identifiers (MCC/MNC or commercial codes) across both feeds; align `billing_period` cutoffs to clearinghouse calendars; adjust static thresholds for large hub partners versus small island operators.
visualization: **Row 1 — KPI tiles**: largest `charge_diff` this period, count of partners over threshold, total `record_gap`, NRTRDE vs TAP lag indicator. **Row 2 — Trend**: timechart of `sum(charge_diff)` by `roaming_partner` per `billing_period`. **Row 3 — Detail table**: `roaming_partner`, `billing_period`, `tap_events`, `cdr_events`, `record_gap`, `charge_diff` with drilldown to `imsi`-level investigative search (policy permitting). **Row 4 — Supplementary**: heatmap partner × period for `record_gap`, plus overlay of clearinghouse ticket IDs from KV lookup.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Communications
3GPP TS 32.297 — CDR File Format and Transfer
GSMA TAP3 Standard — Transferred Account Procedure
ITU-T E.425 — IP Network Performance Objectives

knownFalsePositives: **(1) File sequencing latency** — home rated roaming CDRs often land hours before clearinghouse TAP/RAP batches finish validation; Splunk compares partially loaded partner files and shows artificial record gaps until the release completes. Mitigation: require `tap_file_sequence` completeness flags, widen the billing_period join tolerance by one ingestion bucket, or delay alerts until manifests show **closed**. **(2) Currency rounding and FX pivot timing** — TAP amounts may carry six decimal places whereas internal GSM/5G roaming rating rounds at two decimals, or treasury posts FX pivots days after TAP generation. Mitigation: unify on a pivot currency with published FX tables keyed to clearing date, tolerating micro-epsilon deltas. **(3) Hubbed partners and batched settlement** — roaming hubs aggregate tens of bilateral streams; benign volume crosses naïve static thresholds. Mitigation: per-partner adaptive baselines (median + k×sigma) and standalone hub dashboards. **(4) Retail promotions / zero-rated roaming** — rated events exist while TAP temporarily shows charges awaiting reversal workflows. Mitigation: join `promotional_roam` lookups on IMSI/msisdn + billing_period. **(5) Test IMSI/IR.21 ranges** — inter-operator labs leak into production TAP drops. Mitigation: interconnect-managed exclusion lookups and golden MSISDN allowlists separate from mediation tests. **(6) Clearinghouse reject/resubmit cycles** — release attempts oscillate counts without underlying fraud. Mitigation: ingest `tap_release_attempt`, alert only once finance marks the period cleared, and dedupe natural keys across retries. **(7) MCC/MNC or commercial alias drift** — post-merger partner codes remap while historic keys linger in Splunk lookups. Mitigation: fortnightly alias refresh sourced from interconnect CMDB feeds. **(8) IMS/voice-vs-data granularity mismatch** — data-only PDP/PDU roaming records may lag voice MO/MT TAP lines causing apparent gaps unrelated to leakage. Mitigation: split alerts by `record_type` buckets with tolerances reflecting product mix. **(9) Steered roaming / IREG trials** — temporary steering changes traffic via alternate partners while home CDRs still attribute old partner identifiers mid-test. Mitigation: `steering_calendar` lookups maintained by interconnect product teams. **(10) Statutory mandated roaming charge adjustments** posted weeks later can reopen settled periods legitimately — treat as informational unless breach of signed-off tolerance after regulatory true-up ingestion.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• TAP3/RAP and/or NRTRDE parser outputs with `roaming_partner`, `billing_period`, `charge_amount`, `record_type`, and optional `imsi`/`visited_network`.
• Home-network `cdr:roaming` rated extract with identical partner and period keys (via DB Connect or file).
• Interconnect-maintained **partner alias** lookup (MCC/MNC, commercial codes, hub IDs).
• Heavy forwarder capacity for **large batch files**; disk quarantine for failed parses.
• Legal/privacy review for **`imsi` in dashboards** — mask or aggregate if required by policy.
• License estimate: roaming TAP can add **50–400 GB/day** for international-heavy operators — size index thaw/frozen tiers accordingly.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**TAP / RAP monitor:**

```ini
[monitor:///opt/roaming/TAP/processed/*.cdrtap.csv]
disabled = 0
index = roaming
sourcetype = tap:record
```

If your middleware emits **positional** ASCII, define `DELIMS` or `FIELDALIAS`:

```ini
[tap:record]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
EVAL-roaming_partner = coalesce(roaming_partner, partner_mncc)
EVAL-charge_amount = if(isnull(charge_amount), charge, charge_amount)
```

**NRTRDE** sourcetype `nrtrde:record` with faster poll:

```ini
[monitor:///opt/roaming/NRTRDE/*.lst]
```


**Internal roaming CDR**:

```ini
(DB Connect SQL to mediation roaming rated view → index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:roaming)
```

**Verification:**

```spl
index=roaming sourcetype=tap:record earliest=-24h
| stats count sum(charge_amount) dc(roaming_partner) by billing_period

index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:roaming earliest=-24h
| stats count sum(rated_amount) dc(roaming_partner) by billing_period
```

Align totals within expected **FX** tolerance before building alerts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=roaming sourcetype=tap:record billing_period=<open_or_closed>
| stats count as tap_events, sum(charge_amount) as tap_charges by roaming_partner, billing_period
| join type=left roaming_partner, billing_period [
    search index=cdr sourcetype=cdr:roaming billing_period=<open_or_closed>
    | lookup roaming_partner_alias partner_id as roaming_partner OUTPUT partner_canonical AS roaming_partner
    | stats count as cdr_events, sum(rated_amount) as cdr_charges by roaming_partner, billing_period
  ]
| fillnull value=0 cdr_events cdr_charges
| eval record_gap=tap_events - cdr_events
| eval charge_diff=round(tap_charges - cdr_charges, 2)
| where abs(record_gap) > 100 OR abs(charge_diff) > 1000
| sort - abs(charge_diff)
```

Understanding this SPL
• **tap side `stats`** summarises clearinghouse-derived counts and sums **per partner** and **billing period**, the natural settlement grain.
• **`join type=left`** starts from TAP truth (finance submission basis) and brings in internal rated counts; reversing join changes which gaps surface first — finance usually prefers TAP-anchored.
• **`fillnull`** ensures absent home records still produce **`record_gap`** spikes (missing rating is a defect).
• **Static thresholds** (`100` records, `1000` monetary units) are starter values; replace with:

```spl
| eventstats p90(abs(charge_diff)) as dyn90 by billing_period
| where abs(charge_diff) > max(1000, 1.5*dyn90)
```

when partner mix is heterogeneous.
• **Alias lookup** normalises codes so the join key matches after M&A or hub remaps.
• **Performance**: for huge taps, **summary index** hourly aggregates per partner.

Schedule as Alert: **weekly** for open periods (or **daily** T-7 before clearinghouse cut-off), range **`-30d@d` to `@d`**, throttle **48h** per `roaming_partner,billing_period`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Replay **three historical disputed periods**; confirm known partner gaps reproduce.
(b) Compare **TAP file manifest** row count to Splunk `tap_events` before join.
(c) Validate **FX** by forcing EUR pivot and rerunning.
(d) `| rare roaming_partner` — unexpected partners flag parser field shifts.
(e) Correlation: pick one **high record_gap** partner, drill `imsi` sample — counts should match manual sample.
(f) Role test: interconnect user must not see **raw imsi** if masked at index-time.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard layout as **visualization** spec. Alerting: email + **clearinghouse ticket** webhook; attach **TAP file name** from `source` field.
Runbook: (1) Confirm **no partial file** in quarantine. (2) Check **release number** conflicts. (3) Validate **rated mediation** watermark. (4) Open **hub dispute** within SLA. (5) Update **alias lookup** if partner rename. (6) Document outcome in RA register.

Roaming clearance, TAP3/RAP depth, and partner economics context

Transferred Account Procedure records materialise differently depending on clearinghouse workflows: some operators ingest **converted ASCII CDRTAP** summaries; others ingest **validated RAP settlement lines** prior to treasury payment. Decide whether Splunk anchors on TAP (partner claim) versus internal rated roaming (home assertion). Finance teams overwhelmingly prefer TAP-anchored discrepancy lists because debit/credit chase follows submission deadlines published by hubs like Syniverse GLOBALSTAR or BICS; engineering teams argue from home CDR first. Explicitly encode that policy in dashboards to avoid contradictory incident narratives.

Currency normalisation dominates false positives near quarter-end FX revaluation. Clearinghouses publish FX tables per billing period sometimes **T+48h** late. Maintain an FX ingestion micro-pipeline keyed by TAP **clearing_period** identifiers; without it, benign EUR pivot conversions show false multimillion deltas. Regulatory roaming price caps across certain corridors also inject deterministic differences until retroactive tariff tables arrive—keep government reference IDs in lookups.

Partner hub traffic scales super-linearly versus bilateral partners. Static record thresholds (`record_gap > 100`) will page constantly for hubs while masking micro-partner fraud. Prefer **baseline z-scores**: compute rolling 180d mean and stdev per `roaming_partner` for `tap_events` and escalate when current period exceeds mean + k*sigma. Hub partners merit separate alerting streams with calibrated k.

IMS VoLTE roaming introduces secondary CDR correlators (SDP media, QoS-Class). IMS-specific record types appearing only on one feed may drive apparent gaps unrelated to fraudulent SIM-box usage. Maintain an **IMS_capability** lookup per roaming_partner to widen tolerances briefly after interconnect upgrades.

Operational controls: TAP file renaming conventions often embed **release_attempt** counters; ingest `source` meta as `tap_release_id` extracted from path. Replay scenarios on DR stacks require cryptographic provenance hashes if auditors demand chain-of-custody—hash each source file externally and ingest as indexed field alongside Splunk ingestion.

Compliance: NRTRDE for fraud suppression is **not financially definitive** versus TAP totals; bifurcate dashboards so fraud teams versus finance settlement teams consume context-appropriate KPIs rather than debating a single ambiguous panel.

Throughput: multi-gigabyte TAP drops may exceed forwarder ingestion windows; staged landing with atomic `mv` operations once files complete avoids partial parses. Compress intermediate CSV with snappy—not gzip—for CPU efficiency if converter supports it.
Operational dispute lifecycle and TAP version governance

Beyond Splunk artefacts, dispute evidence numbering must align with GSMA clarification workflows; ingest dispute_ticket from interconnect trackers into dashboards merging legal statuses (open, arbitration) so engineers stop redundant triage once finance counsel owns threads.

Minor TAP revisions change digit masking subtly—coordinate parser GIT commit metadata indexed as tap_parser_build for replay fidelity and regulator inquiry reconstruction.

Separate escalation paths: record_gap spikes route to IT integrity first; monetary charge_diff deltas route interconnect commercial desks first-tax records may bifurcate unless record_type distinguishes surcharges explicitly.

Throughput dup diagnosis: duplicated Universal Forwarder monitor stanzas on one NFS mount double tap_events—investigate stats count by source early.

Historical regulatory roaming cap true-ups resurfacing settled rows need financial calendar lookups requiring human acknowledgement before stale cleared disputes reopen automatically.

Fleet 5G SA roaming introduces PDP context shifts; maintain transitional tolerance lookups until TAP harmonises NSA and SA artefacts.
Clearinghouse alignment, TAP duplicates, and treasury hand-offs

Encode each clearinghouse deadline and hub identifier in lookups so TAP versus home-rated comparisons honour the finance calendar—not only `_time`. Misaligned fiscal windows dominate benign false positives in settlement reconciliation.

Treat clearinghouse retries as first-class metadata: ingest `tap_release_attempt` and sequence numbers—use them to reconcile legitimate resubmissions versus accidental double ingestion. Natural keys assembled from IMSI fragments, timestamps, roaming record types, and charge elements support deduplication summaries feeding this UC.

Treasury forecasting may ingest daily Splunk aggregates of partner `charge_diff` distributions—even small statistical signals improve liquidity buffers during volatile roaming seasons.

Hub migrations freeze alerting for impacted partners via time-bounded lookup until triple-pass reconciliation clears the migration gate.

Retroactive regulatory roaming refunds should carry `reg_reference_id`; suppress stale ticket reopen automation unless acknowledgement toggled in KV.

Throughput: regional split of HF parsers reduces backpressure contagion across continents.

Separate saved searches for NRTRDE security posture versus TAP settlement KPIs—even within one index—to avoid organisational mixed messaging across fraud and interconnect finance stakeholders.
Appendix — TAP file integrity, partner baselines, and regulatory overlays

Hash incoming TAP source files at the edge (`sha256`) and index as `tap_file_hash`; on re-ingestion with identical hash Splunk should short-circuit duplication using `dedup` keyed on `source`+`hash` in generation logic. When hashes differ but filenames repeat, treat as genuine resubmission worthy of finance review.

Maintain **90-day rolling baseline** `stats` per partner for `tap_events` median and IQR; escalate when current period exceeds median + 5×IQR instead of static `100` threshold for record gaps—this scales hubs vs islands automatically.

Some regulators require **privacy masking** of IMSI in long-term indexes while analyses need full precision—dual-route ingestion (masked for general, tokenised join for controlled break-glass summary index) satisfies both when architected deliberately with legal sign-off.

Operationalise **interconnect ticket** deep links: KV store mapping `roaming_partner` to ServiceNow interconnect queue shortens mean time to dispute filing measurably.

When internal rating uses **Tax Determination** engines external to mediation, ensure tax lines split in TAP comparators or charge_diff misleads—extend props with `tax_indicator` field exclusions for core alert comparators.
Dispute aging analytics and partner clustering

Track **days-to-resolve** from first Splunk discrepancy to finance closure code; segment by `hub_id` vs bilateral—process bottlenecks differ and deserve distinct improvement OKRs.

Cluster partners by traffic covariance using simple `correlate` analyses monthly—unexpected cluster shifts often precede TAP parser field misalignments affecting whole geographies simultaneously.

When disputes reopen retroactively, annotate `reopen_reason` indexed for trend analysis—helps distinguish systemic parser bugs from one-off commercial settlements.

Use **dynamic count gap thresholds** based on partner size: `threshold_records = max(100, 0.005 * tap_events_baseline)` blended percentile and floor model documented in runbook.

Maintain hot-warm-cold paths for TAP archives with legal hold tags preventing premature frozen deletion during active arbitration windows extending years occasionally.
Charset and encoding safeguards for TAP/CDR pipelines

Treat encoding mis-declarations as ingestion incidents: compute rolling averages of `len(_raw)` per `source` or `roaming_partner` when trailing monetary tokens truncate statistically. Normalize to UTF-8 in pick-up scripts before Splunk; positional TAP ASCII with national characters silently corrupts decimals under mistaken Latin-1 declarations.

Replay golden TAP fixtures via CI applying the identical delimiter rules Splunk uses; clustered geographic divergences often indicate middleware upgrades, not roaming fraud.

Retain immutable archives of originals while Splunk consumes checksum-verified ASCII transforms (`file_sha` per batch).

Escalate suspected encoding regressions with `fieldsummary`, sample corrupted rows, and connector logs—facts beat war-room anecdotes when SLA clocks burn.

Bridging RACI, tabletops, and summary-index economics

Formalise RACI per discrepancy class so Splunk paging routes automatically: ingestion integrity faults go to mediation operations; TAP layout upgrades go to interconnect engineering with vendor TAM; disputed monetary deltas require commercial interconnect plus treasury observers; NRTRDE-only spikes route to fraud operations instead of settlement leads.

Quarterly tabletop exercises replay sanitised TAP vs CDR divergence timelines from Splunk cloned indexes—new hires rehearse sequencing (hash manifests, FX tables, alias lookup freshness, clearinghouse ticketing) without touching production finance locks.

Economise SH CPU using hourly `summarize`/summary-index layers emitting `billing_period`,`roaming_partner`,`tap_events`,`cdr_events`,`tap_charges`,`cdr_charges`—hourly deltas catch drift early while nightly heavy reconciles reconcile penny-level residuals.

Cold storage: TAP binaries should live on WORM object storage outside Splunk; ingest only metadata pointers (`tape_archive_uri`) referencing frozen artifacts for auditors demanding chain-of-custody—not optional in some regulatory settlements.

Operational analytics: correlate Splunk discrepancy detection timestamps with ServiceNow interconnect queue aging—reveals human process bottlenecks distinct from ingestion quality.

Retro roaming caps documented in lookups prevent automated dispute reopen churn when governments publish delayed adjustments—annotate expected directional bias transparently.

IMSI truncation for privacy must match rating extract transformations exactly—silent key mismatch creates catastrophic false positives; test truncations nightly using synthetic IMSI fixtures.

Executive weekly digest aggregates open partner deltas by aging bucket using Splunk lookups to ticket states—aligns KPI narrative to tooling maturity realities.

Innovation restraint: flashy blockchain timestamping seldom beats rigorous SHA manifests plus ITSM ticketing—prioritise pragmatic integrity controls first.
Configuration versioning: snapshot `macros.conf`, `savedsearches.conf`, and Alert Manager policies in Git whenever settlement, churn, or voice correlation thresholds change—auditors and regression testing both require reproducible baselines tied to CFO sign-off quarters.Search-time macro hygiene: document owning team, escalation path, last tabletop date, dependent sourcetypes, and wiki deep links directly in macros so rotating engineers cannot resurrect dormant misconfigurations unknowingly.
Latency note: Splunk indexing lag on multi-gig TAP drops can exceed clearinghouse SLA if forwarder queues saturate—monitor `splunkd` queue metrics and escalate capacity before interpretation mistakes attribute ingestion delay to bilateral fraud.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **CDR events always zero after join** — alias mismatch; reload lookup.
• **Charge_diff spikes on hub only** — expected batching; use dynamic thresholds.
• **Duplicates inflate tap_events** — dedup `natural_key` if parser double-emits on retries.
• **NRTRDE diverges but TAP aligns** — normal; split alerts per feed purpose.
• **Join timeout** — reduce period scope; summary index TAP side.
• **Currency explosions** — wrong FX table date; align to **TAP clearing date**.
• **Sudden truncation** — file encoding change; revisit CHARSET and LINE_BREAKER in props.
grandmaExplanation: When our customers use their phones abroad, we swap detailed bills with partner phone companies. We compare their version and ours before money changes hands so nobody pays twice and small mistakes do not explode into fights.
telcoUseCase: Roaming settlement — cross-check TAP/NRTRDE files from partner operators against home CDRs to catch inter-carrier billing disputes before clearinghouse deadlines.
